title: Morelosia spathulata
intro: Morelosia spathulata, also known as Mexican gentian, is a plant species in the Gentianaceae family, characterized by its unique spathulate leaves and vibrant purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including gastrointestinal issues and fever, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Morelosia spathulata include gentiopicrin, gentianine, and amarogentin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to aid digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion.
